creator: brooks@FreeBSD.org
text:
Created attachment 152608
/etc/nsswitch.conf from vm

On a system with nss_ldap deployed, the second "freebsd-update install" command starts failing part way through (install command segfaults).  At the end, all programs that do user/group name resolution fail due to nss segfaulting.

Workaround: If nss_ldap is removed from nsswitch.conf during the install then the install completes and the ldap entries can be re-added.

If I instrument freebsd-update to output the type and path of each thing to be installed/created then I see that the failures start when libthr is installed.  Since the failures are of the install program, this is catastrophic and results in an unusable system with most files not updated.

f /lib/libalias.so.7 
f /lib/libavl.so.2 
f /lib/libbegemot.so.4 
f /lib/libbsdxml.so.4 
f /lib/libc.so.7 
f /lib/libcam.so.6 
f /lib/libcrypt.so.5 
f /lib/libcrypto.so.7 
f /lib/libctf.so.2 
f /lib/libcxxrt.so.1 
f /lib/libdevstat.so.7 
f /lib/libdtrace.so.2 
f /lib/libedit.so.7 
f /lib/libgcc_s.so.1 
f /lib/libgeom.so.5 
f /lib/libipsec.so.4 
f /lib/libipx.so.5 
f /lib/libjail.so.1 
f /lib/libkiconv.so.4 
f /lib/libkvm.so.6 
f /lib/libm.so.5 
f /lib/libmd.so.6 
f /lib/libncurses.so.8 
f /lib/libncursesw.so.8 
f /lib/libnvpair.so.2 
f /lib/libpcap.so.8 
f /lib/libreadline.so.8 
f /lib/libsbuf.so.6 
f /lib/libssp.so.0 
f /lib/libthr.so.3 
f /lib/libufs.so.6 
Segmentation fault (core dumped)
f /lib/libulog.so.0 
Segmentation fault (core dumped)

This all suggests that the new libthr's ABI is some how incompatible with threaded nss modules until a later library is installed.

To aid debugging I have created a VM where I did the following:

- Install 10.0 amd64 from CD
- Upgrade to 10.0-RELAESE-p17 with freebsd-update
- Run "freebsd-update -r 10.1-RELEASE" upgrade
- Run first "freebsd-update install" and reboot
- Install nss_ldap and openldap-server packages
- Configure slapd to allow read access to all and start it
- Configure nss_ldap to use the local base dn (it defaults to localhost)
- Add "group: files ldap" and "passwd: files ldap" to /etc/nsswitch.conf
- Modified freebsd-update to add "echo "$TYPE $FPATH $LINK"" to install_from_index.
- Run "freebsd-update install"
- *BOOM*

A tarball of the vmware fusion 6 VM (1.3GB!) is at https://people.freebsd.org/~brooks/stuff/fbsd-10.0-10.1-boom.vmwarevm.tar.xz

VM Notes: root password is empty.  Remove ldap from nsswitch.conf before rebooting or slapd will hang.

We need to:
 a) Publish an errata telling people to remove third party nss modules when running freebsd-update (I've seen reports of both ldap and winbind breaking)
 b) Find the underlying cause.
 c) Find a way to work around this issue in freebsd-update.
